articles.title: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations
articles.summary: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.

articles.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drone technology
articles.summary: The technology, developed under the Aukus military pact, is aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defence.

articles.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
articles.summary: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

articles.summary: People have just begun returning to check on their homes in the block that was hit early on Friday morning.

articles.title: Italy bans Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
articles.summary: Local authorities say the cancellation of other West concerts had played a role in the decision.

articles.title: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
articles.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

articles.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave after 10 days
articles.summary: Two of the seven villagers who entered the narrow tunnels in search for gold on 20 May are still missing.

articles.title: Spain's Sánchez digs in after eight years as PM as wave of scandals threatens survival
articles.summary: Corruption probes of colleagues and relatives have put Spain's premier into a fight for survival.

articles.title: Trump attacks artists dropping out of US Freedom 250 concert and mulls appearing himself
articles.summary: Performers Young MC, Poison frontman Bret Michaels and country singer Martina McBride raised concerns the event will be political.
